user_outcome: An operator invoking sworn maintainability review reaches a NUL-safe, binary-safe committed-Git boundary and receives a redacted fail-closed gate before unavailable semantic-scope authority can act.
rationale: This slice is the sole low-level Git boundary for semantic scope and later integration. It returns raw committed identities rather than parsing human-oriented output, invokes git with argv rather than a shell, never logs blobs, and confines every path to the repository. There is no PII, network protocol, UI, or accessibility surface beyond deterministic errors. Path/object enumeration and history walks are linear in Git output; callers, not this layer, own Baton policy.
in_scope: Add typed committed tree-entry and blob APIs returning exact repo-relative path bytes, mode, object type, object ID, and unmodified blob bytes; parse -z/NUL-delimited Git output and preserve unusual whitespace, tabs, newlines, Unicode, symlinks, submodules, and binary blobs.

Add first-parent commit/parent enumeration, merge-base, ancestry, exact tree lookup, and commit-to-path/blob history operations that distinguish absent objects, shallow history, command failure, and a legitimate false ancestry result.

Wrap git merge-file --object-id over explicit base/ours/theirs committed blobs and return the canonical result object ID, clean/conflict disposition, and stderr-safe error without consulting worktree attributes, filters, custom drivers, or hand-resolved bytes.

Provide clean worktree/index preflight, atomic staging of an explicit confined path set, commit identity retrieval, and exact destination-ref push with expected-source verification for later restart-safe command transactions.

Use one argv-based runner with an explicit repository directory, -- separators where paths follow revisions, bounded output parsing, and typed exit-status handling; reject empty repository roots, NUL-bearing inputs, option-like unsafe refs, and non-confined paths before execution.

Prove the exported Git boundary against real temporary SHA-1 and, when supported, SHA-256 repositories with adversarial filenames, binary bytes, merge commits, shallow-history fixtures, conflicts, clean/dirty state, and a local bare remote.

Introduce the already-planned sworn maintainability review public command as a fail-closed scaffold: it validates protocol policy and the committed-Git boundary, then exits non-zero with the redacted structured reason semantic_scope_unavailable until S07 supplies that gate. It performs no model dispatch, report write, commit, or push.
out_of_scope: Candidate-path selection, canonical exclusions, normalized diffs, manifests, and fingerprints belong to S07-canonical-semantic-scope.
Report, lifecycle, freshness, rollback, shared-touchpoint, and integration-ready semantics belong to S08 through S12.
This package must not import internal/maintainability, internal/integration, board, state, protocol policy, model, command, or MCP packages.
Network transports other than Git's later caller-directed push to an already configured ref are not added; credentials and remote URLs are never logged.

effort_complexity.rationale: The API surface is broad across eleven files but each operation has a documented Git plumbing contract and deterministic temporary-repository proof.

risks.description: Line-oriented parsing corrupts valid Git paths or binary content and changes semantic identity.
risks.mitigation: Use -z output and length-safe byte parsing end to end; include newline, tab, leading dash, Unicode, symlink, submodule, and NUL-containing blob fixtures.

risks.description: Ref or path input is interpreted as a Git option or shell expression.
risks.mitigation: Never invoke a shell, validate refs with Git's check-ref-format/rev-parse rules, use -- separators, and reject empty roots or NUL before spawning.

risks.description: Canonical merge-file or push behavior depends on ambient Git configuration.
risks.mitigation: Operate on explicit object IDs and explicit destination refs, clear relevant config in fixtures, and repeat proofs under hostile attributes, filters, merge drivers, and push defaults.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the raw Git boundary reads a real repository containing adversarial filenames, binary blobs, symlinks, submodules, and both object formats THE SYSTEM SHALL return exact path bytes, modes, object types, object IDs, and blob bytes without line-splitting or normalization (N-03, N-04, N-06, N-07, N-08, N-10).

WHEN callers request first-parent history, commit parents, ancestry, merge-base, tree entries, or a record's introducing path/blob commit THE SYSTEM SHALL distinguish valid false/absent outcomes from command errors and fail explicitly on shallow or missing required history.

WHEN merge-file receives three committed blob OIDs under hostile attributes, filters, and custom merge drivers THE SYSTEM SHALL return Git's configuration-independent --object-id result and clean/conflict status, never worktree or manually resolved bytes.

IF repository root, ref, object, path, cleanliness, staging, commit, expected source, or exact destination-ref push precondition is invalid THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed non-success result before performing the next mutation and SHALL redact blob bytes, credentials, and remote URLs from errors.

WHEN the built sworn binary runs maintainability review against a clean temporary repository with committed adversarial paths THE SYSTEM SHALL reach the real typed Git boundary, preserve exact path/object identities, then exit non-zero with redacted reason semantic_scope_unavailable and zero model dispatch, report write, commit, or push; IF Git identity or cleanliness is invalid THEN it SHALL fail at that earlier gate (C-01, N-03, N-04, N-06, N-07, N-08, N-10).
